channel.title: CyberWatch Threat Intelligence
channel.description: Aggregated CVEs, advisories, incidents and IOCs

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: kernel-7.1.3-101.fc43 (FEDORA-2026-085c9e92a4)
item.description: The 7.1.3-101/201 builds contain an important fix for XFS filesystem users.

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: kernel-7.1.3-201.fc44 (FEDORA-2026-e8e294a7fa)
item.description: The 7.1.3-101/201 builds contain an important fix for XFS filesystem users.

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: spoofdpi-1.5.3-1.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-b382f3a576)
item.description: Automatic update for spoofdpi-1.5.3-1.fc45. ##### **Changelog** ``` * Tue Jul 14 2026 Emir Akdag - 1.5.3-1 - Update spoofdpi to 1.5.3 - Update to 1.5.3 to fix CVE-2026-27145 - Add missing license for go-localereader - Fix build directory case sensitivity issue - Resolves: rhbz#2494220, rhbz#2494389 ```

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: firefox-152.0.6-1.fc44 (FEDORA-2026-51c2920764)
item.description: - New upstream release (152.0.6)

item.description: Ruby 4.0.6 is released.

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fedora: mupdf-1.27.2-4.fc45 (FEDORA-2026-334a616b71)
item.description: Automatic update for mupdf-1.27.2-4.fc45. ##### **Changelog** ``` * Thu Jul 16 2026 Michael J Gruber - 1.27.2-4 - fix CVE-2026-7233 (rhbz#2463402) ```

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-3180
item.description: Complete exploitation toolkit for CVE-2026-3180 - WordPress Contest Gallery SQL Injection vulnerability. Features automated data extraction, WAF bypass, reverse shell, SQLMap integration, Burp extension generation, and reporting for penetration testing and security research.

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-53359
item.description: KVM KernelCare + Januscape (CVE-2026-53359) verification & mitigation scripts

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-57821
item.description: 🔐 CVE-2026-57821 - Apache Fineract SQL Injection Toolkit (Educational) 📚 Two Python scripts for authorized security testing: verifier.py (safe detection, no extraction) and exploit.py (deep analysis). Supports 11 DB types. Perfect for understanding SQL injection vulnerabilities. ⚠️ for educational & research purposes only.

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-46726
item.description: Reproducer for CVE-2026-46726: Apache Camel camel-vertx-websocket unfiltered inbound header injection enabling SSRF and secret disclosure via property-placeholder resolution (fixed in 4.14.8/4.18.3/4.21.0)

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-20896
item.description: this is a modified POC of rz1027 for CVE-2026-20896

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-43499
item.description: CVE-2026-43499 exploit reproduction on jinghu (Xiaomi Pad 7 Ultra)

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-34197
item.description: CVE-2026-34197 - Apache ActiveMQ Jolokia Remote Code Execution (RCE)

item.title: [CRITICAL] PoC released: CVE-2026-14894
item.description: Super Forms Unauthenticated File Upload RCE | CVSS 9.8

item.title: [MEDIUM] PoC released: CVE-2023-25690
item.description: HTTP Request Smuggling lab: Apache 2.4.55 CRLF injection

item.title: [HIGH] PoC released: CVE-2026-33017
item.description: CVE-2026-33017 Exploit | by infrar3d

item.description: Ransomware group 'interlock' claimed victim: Converting Equipment International. Country: GB. Sector: Manufacturing.

item.title: [MEDIUM] Arch Linux: Arch Issue
item.description: Type: | Severity: Unknown | Package:

item.title: [MEDIUM] VMware: VMSA-2026-0004: VMware Cloud Foundation Operations updates address multiple vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-41722, CVE-2026-41723 and CVE-2026-41724)
[MEDIUM] CVE-2026-21383: Cryptographic Issue when using a static initialization vector for AES-GCM key wr...
item.description: Cryptographic Issue when using a static initialization vector for AES-GCM key wrapping, which requires a unique value for each call to ensure security.

item.title: [LOW] CVE-2026-21379: Memory Corruption when allocating memory with sizes that exceed the maximum allo...
item.description: Memory Corruption when allocating memory with sizes that exceed the maximum allowed value.

item.title: [LOW] CVE-2026-21370: Memory Corruption when validating input batch size and buffer plane count exceed...
item.description: Memory Corruption when validating input batch size and buffer plane count exceeds maximum allowed values.

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-21369: Memory Corruption when handling flash commands due to outdated LED count values ...
item.description: Memory Corruption when handling flash commands due to outdated LED count values being used after userspace modification.

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2026-21368: Memory Corruption when parsing jpeg commands due to unaccounted extra writes to ...
item.description: Memory Corruption when parsing jpeg commands due to unaccounted extra writes to the buffer during validation checks.

item.title: [LOW] CVE-2026-14471: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in the metrics-service retention pol...
item.description:
Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in the metrics-service retention policy management component in Amazon mcp-gateway-registry before 1.0.13 might allow an authenticated remote user to execute arbitrary SQL queries via a crafted table_name value that is interpolated into SQL statements in identifier position.
To remediate this issue, users should upgrade to version 1.0.13 or later.

item.title: [CRITICAL] CVE-2026-14468: HashiCorp Terraform Enterprise contained an issue in its version control system ...
item.description: HashiCorp Terraform Enterprise contained an issue in its version control system (VCS) ingestion of registry modules that did not correctly enforce the intended boundary on packaged module content. This may allow an authenticated user to include files from outside the intended repository content in a module and then download them, potentially exposing sensitive files readable by the ingestion proce

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2025-59617: Memory Corruption when processing multiple IOCTL calls with the same buffer file...
item.description: Memory Corruption when processing multiple IOCTL calls with the same buffer file descriptor input.

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2025-59616: Memory Corruption when processing multiple IOCTL calls with the same buffer file...
item.description: Memory Corruption when processing multiple IOCTL calls with the same buffer file descriptor input due to accessing already freed memory.

item.title: [MEDIUM] CVE-2025-59615: Memory Corruption when invoking device input/output control operations for mappi...
item.description: Memory Corruption when invoking device input/output control operations for mapping and unmapping persistent memory buffers due to improper synchronization.

item.title: [MEDIUM] Fake crypto scams try to piggyback off SpaceX IPO
item.description: Scammers are exploiting public interest in the SpaceX IPO through fraudulent investment portals impersonating SpaceX, Elon Musk, and major financial brands including Fidelity and Robinhood. The campaign uses themed domains to lure victims into fake onboarding processes that mimic legitimate investment procedures, including W-8BEN tax forms for non-U.S. investors. Victims are asked to select invest

item.title: [MEDIUM] WebAssembly Malware Found in Trojanized Open VSX Extensions
item.description: Trojanized Visual Studio Code extensions distributed via the Open VSX marketplace deliver a sophisticated WebAssembly-based attack chain. The extensions ship ChaCha20-encrypted TinyGo-compiled WebAssembly modules that poll the Solana blockchain for command-and-control instructions embedded in transaction memos. This novel dead-drop technique allows attackers to rotate infrastructure without hardco

item.title: [MEDIUM] Investigation of email-based attack delivering MediaFire ZIP file with execution chain analysis
item.description: An investigation revealed a malicious email campaign directing victims to download a ZIP file from MediaFire. The infection chain began with a Python setup executable (Setu.exe) that side-loaded a malicious 400 MB python37.dll containing repeated byte padding. The DLL performed process injection into dllhost.exe, establishing communication with a C2 server at 138.124.186.2:7000. The threat actor d

item.title: [MEDIUM] Introducing CylindricalCanine: The GoldenEyeDog subgroup responsible for the April DigiCert incident
item.description: Chinese cybercrime group GoldenEyeDog has been active since 2015, regularly updating malware and leveraging code-signing certificates to bypass Windows SmartScreen since 2024. A subgroup called CylindricalCanine uses Golden Gh0st Loader and Golden Gh0st RAT, modified versions of the 2008 Gh0st RAT, primarily targeting finance organizations in the Asia Pacific region through phishing campaigns. In

item.title: [MEDIUM] ​​Kratos PhaaS Targets US and EU: How to Reduce Microsoft 365 Account Takeover Risk​
item.description: Kratos is a mature Phishing-as-a-Service operation targeting Microsoft 365 users across the United States, Europe, and other regions. The platform enables attackers to steal credentials through trusted platforms, anti-bot verification, and convincing login pages. Researchers traced three generations of the kit and uncovered 1,484 previously unattributed detonations. The operation targets organizat

item.title: [MEDIUM] TELEPUZ: a modular MaaS malware spreading via CLICKFIX-VIDAR chains
item.description: TELEPUZ is a newly emerged modular malware-as-a-service first detected in April 2026, spreading through CLICKFIX-VIDAR infection chains. The lightweight, full-featured threat employs sophisticated evasion techniques including indirect syscalls, NTDLL unhooking, and anti-VM checks. It establishes persistence through service installation, communicates via WebSockets with C2 servers, and downloads ad

item.title: [MEDIUM] Unpacking the AsyncAPI npm supply chain compromise and import-time payload delivery
item.description: On July 14, 2026, a coordinated supply chain attack compromised the @asyncapi npm organization, affecting five package versions across four packages. The attack originated from a GitHub Actions workflow vulnerability that exposed privileged credentials, enabling unauthorized code injection. Unlike typical postinstall attacks, this campaign executes at module import time, bypassing common npm insta

item.title: [MEDIUM] Gamers beware: malicious wallpapers on Steam found stealing accounts
item.description: Since late 2025, cybercriminals have been exploiting Wallpaper Engine, a popular live wallpaper application on Steam, to distribute malware through Steam Workshop. Attackers target primarily Chinese and Russian gamers by embedding malicious code within application wallpapers shared on the platform. These compromised wallpapers deliver various malware types including infostealers, backdoors, crypto

item.title: [MEDIUM] Potemkin Loader & RMMProject The Anatomy of a ClickFix Attack
item.description: A ClickFix social engineering attack on an unmonitored endpoint led to a multi-stage intrusion affecting over 11 hosts. The infection chain began with a malicious HTA payload that silently installed an MSI package containing Potemkin, a custom loader with a deterministic DGA. Potemkin delivered RMMProject, a 4.4 MB Lua-scriptable RAT featuring browser credential theft with Chrome App-Bound Encrypt

item.title: [CRITICAL] Attackers Weaponize Microsoft Teams Relays to Stay Hidden
item.description: Attackers deploying DragonForce ransomware against a major U.S. services firm concealed their command-and-control traffic within Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure using Backdoor.Turn, a custom Go-based remote access trojan. This novel technique leverages anonymous Teams visitor tokens and TURN relay servers to mask malicious communications as legitimate Microsoft traffic. The intrusion lasted o

item.title: [MEDIUM] Spamhaus DROP: 1.10.16.0/20
item.description: Malicious IP range: 1.10.16.0/20 — SBL256894

item.description: C2 server: 50.16.16.211:443 | Malware: QakBot | Status: online | Hostname: ec2-50-16-16-211.compute-1.amazonaws.com

item.title: [MEDIUM] Feodo C2: 162.243.103.246:8080 (Emotet)
item.description: C2 server: 162.243.103.246:8080 | Malware: Emotet | Status: offline

item.title: [MEDIUM] GHSA: ArcadeDB has cross-database IDOR: /ts/*, /batch/*, Prometheus and Grafana handlers bypass authorization
item.description: About 14 HTTP handlers resolve the {database} path param and call getDatabase(...) WITHOUT user.canAccessToDatabase(...) and without setting the engine principal, because they extend AbstractServerHttpHandler directly instead of DatabaseAbstractHandler (which holds the only per-database gate at DatabaseAbstractHandler.java:69,88-90). Affected: PostBatchHandler:129, PostTimeSeriesWriteHandler:115,

item.title: [MEDIUM] GHSA: ArcadeDB: Scripting authorization gate (GHSA-48qw-824m-86pr) bypassed via SQL DEFINE FUNCTION ... LANGUAGE js
item.description: The GHSA-48qw-824m-86pr hardening added a checkPermissionsOnDatabase(UPDATE_SECURITY) gate on the polyglot engine (PolyglotQueryEngine.java:112-114,126,176,199), but only there. The SQL route to JavaScript never touches it: DefineFunctionStatement.executeSimple (DefineFunctionStatement.java:37-100), LocalSchema.registerFunctionLibrary, and SQLQueryEngine library-function invocation (SQLQueryEngine

item.title: [CRITICAL] GHSA: ArcadeDB: Trigger scripts run with java.lang.* allowed, enabling OS command execution (RCE)
item.description: ScriptTriggerExecutor sets allowedPackages to java.lang.*, java.util.*, java.time.*, java.math.* (ScriptTriggerExecutor.java:56); trigger creation is gated only at UPDATE_SCHEMA (LocalSchema.createTrigger:636). Permitting java.lang.* host-class lookup lets a trigger script do Java.type("java.lang.Runtime").getRuntime().exec(...) (or ProcessBuilder). The reflection denylist does not block Java.type
